document.heading: Anacostia Business Improvement District Amendment Act of 2026

To amend the Business Improvement Districts Act of 1996 to permit the Mayor to approve a petition by the Anacostia BID to expand its geographic area to include exempt real property owners, including real property owned by the District of Columbia.

history.summary: BILL SUMMARY - As introduced, Bill 26-247 would permit the Mayor to approve a petition by the Anacostia BID to expand its geographic area to include exempt real property owners.

document.text: BE IT ENACTED BY THE COUNCIL OF THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA, That this act may be cited as the "Anacostia Business Improvement District Amendment Act of 2026".

section.text: Section 10 of the Business Improvement Districts Act of 1996, effective May 29, 1996 (D.C. Law 11-134; D.C. Official Code § 2-1215.09), is amended by adding a new subsection (d) to read as follows:

para.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this act, the Mayor may approve a petition by the Anacostia BID to expand its geographic area to include real property owned by the District of Columbia adjacent to or abutting the Anacostia BID's geographical area as delineated in section 209; provided, that the expanded BID's geographic area shall not become effective until the effective date of an act of the Council approving such BID geographic expansion; and provided further, that initial BID taxes for such expanded area, to the extent it includes taxable property, shall be collected at the next practicable regularly scheduled billing pursuant to section 16.

section.heading: Fiscal impact statement.
section.text: The Council adopts the fiscal impact statement in the committee report as the fiscal impact statement required by section 4a of the General Legislative Procedures Act of 1975, approved October 16, 2006 (120 Stat. 2038; D. C. Official Code § 1-301.47a).

section.heading: Effective date.
section.text: This act shall take effect following approval by the Mayor (or in the event of veto by the Mayor, action by the Council to override the veto) and a 30-day period of congressional review as provided in section 602(c)(1) of the District of Columbia Home Rule Act, approved December 24, 1973 (87 Stat. 813; D.C. Official Code § 1-206.02(c)(1)).
